title: Frequently Asked Questions
text:
Should the diagnostic code DS098C Let hovedtraume (GCS 14–15) be used according to the definition in the Scandinavian Neurotrauma Committee (SNC) guidelines?
No. The code should be applied to all patients with head trauma and a Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) score of 14–15 at the time of admission, including those who would be classified as “minimal” according to the SNC guidelines.

Which patients belong in the category “øget blødningstendens”?
“Øget blødningstendens” refers to patients with alcohol abuse, liver disease, or bleeding disorders - not those receiving anticoagulant (AK) therapy, which is listed as a separate risk factor.

What if we cannot measure S100B?
All participating emergency departments can, as a general rule, measure S100B. If S100B is temporarily unavailable or more than 6 hours have passed since the injury, a CT scan should be performed (and the patient discharged if normal). Observation is only required if the CT scan is abnormal.

title: Protocol
text:
Study Overview








Item
Details




Study name
HEADWISE - Head Trauma Evaluation Algorithm for Deploying Warranted Imaging Safely and Efficiently


Objective
Evaluate the diagnostic performance and safety profile of a new Choosing Wisely algorithm for MHT management, aiming to reduce CT overuse.


Study Sites
Aalborg, Aarhus, Esbjerg, Kolding, Aabenraa, Odense, Slagelse, Herlev, Bispebjerg, Hvidovre


Study Period
01.XX.202X – 01.XX.202X


Primary Endpoint
Proportion undergoing neurosurgery within 10 days of trauma


Secondary Endpoints
All-cause and cause-specific mortality
Readmission-free survival
Time from trauma to neurosurgery
Rate of intracranial haemorrhage
Duration of hospitalization and ICU admission if applicable
Number of CT scans, possible neurology admission, and neurorehabilitation
Economic consequences including elective CTs and sickness benefit


Follow-up
Three months





Roles and Responsibilities




Role
Person
Responsibilities




Principal Investigator
Rozh Al-Mashhadi
Overall study oversight, data quality


Local Investigator
See Team
Daily workflow management, staff training


Local Project Manager
See Team
Aid the local site investigator in quality assurance


Data Collector
Julie Bjørn Bay
Data extraction from registers


Clinician
Any doctor at admission site
Patient screening, patient information, guideline adherence, code assignment


Statistician

Data analysis plan

title: Deploy Site
text:
# Render full site
quarto render .

# Only commit if there are changes
if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
  git add -f docs
  git commit -m "Site update: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
  git push
else
  echo "No changes to commit"
fi

title: HEADWISE
text:
Head Trauma Evaluation Algorithm for Deploying
Warranted Imaging Safely and Efficient



A multicenter cluster-randomized trial




Minor head trauma is common in emergency departments
Approximately half of patients undergo CT scanning
The vast majority of scans are without relevant findings
Only 0–1% result in treatment

We hypothesize
that choosing wisely can safely reduce CT use by 25–30%

title: Trial Procedures
text:
Local Visits
The Organizing Committee will visit the 10 participating departments ahead of trial start to inform thoroughly about the study. Posters and flyers will be distributed. The Organizing Committee will also visit the departments several times during the trial period, including at the time of transition.
